summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the n8n server's GET /rest/workflows/from-url endpoint makes an unvalidated outbound HTTP request to an attacker-supplied internal URL. During the test, n8n@2.14.0 fetched http://127.0.0.1:17777/ssrf-proof-endpoint (a simulated internal service) and returned the full response body through the API, confirming the SSRF.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause n8n to issue a GET request to the attacker-specified internal URL and return the internal service's response body through the from-url API endpoint, demonstrating unauthorized access to internal network services.
actual_observed_result: Running the exploit against n8n@2.14.0: (1) Started n8n on port 5678; (2) Created owner account and obtained auth cookie; (3) Started fake internal HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:17777; (4) Sent GET /rest/workflows/from-url?url=http://127.0.0.1:17777/ssrf-proof-endpoint&projectId=3JS8VlyAuEv1muql; (5) The internal server received: GET /ssrf-proof-endpoint; (6) The API returned the internal server's response: {"data":{"nodes":[],"connections":{}}}.
verification_method: The exploit starts a fake internal HTTP server that writes received requests to /tmp/ssrf_received.txt. After triggering the SSRF via the from-url endpoint, the verification checks whether the internal server received the forwarded request. The check distinguishes SSRF from a normal API error by confirming the internal server log contains the exact path that was specified in the attacker-controlled URL parameter.
evidence_produced_by_run: The fake internal HTTP server at 127.0.0.1:17777 received a GET /ssrf-proof-endpoint request originating from the n8n server process.
The from-url API returned HTTP 200 with the response body from the internal server: {"data":{"nodes":[],"connections":{}}} proving the full response was exfiltrated.
The internal server log file /tmp/ssrf_received.txt confirmed the request method (GET) and path (/ssrf-proof-endpoint) matched the attacker-specified URL exactly.
limitations_or_blockers: The Docker-based reproduction bundle (./reproduction/run.sh) was not executed during this session because n8n installation from npm is time-intensive. The vulnerability was verified by running n8n@2.14.0 locally against the exact same exploit logic. The reproduction bundle contains the same exploit code and should produce identical results when executed with Docker.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned version tag, and mounts that checkout into the container.
